results.content:
22:A total of 606 patients (32 % female; 37 % African, 33 % Asian; n = 311 EFV400, n = 295 EFV600) were included. EFV PK parameters, including C12, were not associated with pVL <200 copies/mL at 96 weeks (odds ratio [OR] 5.25, 95 % confidence interval [CI] 0.41–67.90, p = 0.204). Lower risk of CNS-related adverse events was associated with CYP2B6 983TC/CC (OR 0.35, 95 % CI 0.15–0.81, p = 0.015) and higher risk was associated with CYP2B6 15582CT/TT and ABCB1 3435TT (OR 1.46, 95 % CI 1.02–2.09, p = 0.040; OR 2.31, 95 % CI 1.33–4.02, p = 0.003, respectively). Discontinuation due to adverse events (clinician decision) was independently associated with dose (OR 2.54, 95 % CI 1.19–5.43, p = 0.016). C12 between 0.47 and 0.76 mg/L provided sensitivity/specificity >90 % (100 %/92.3 to 98.9 %/92.3 %) for achieving pVL <200 copies/mL at 96 weeks.
40:A total of 606 patients (32 % female; 37 % African, 33 % Asian; *n* *=* 311 EFV400, *n* *=* 295 EFV600) were included. EFV PK parameters, including C_12_, were not associated with pVL <200 copies/mL at 96 weeks (odds ratio [OR] 5.25, 95 % confidence interval [CI] 0.41–67.90, *p* *=* 0.204). Lower risk of CNS-related adverse events was associated with *CYP2B6* 983TC/CC (OR 0.35, 95 % CI 0.15–0.81, *p* *=* 0.015) and higher risk was associated with *CYP2B6* 15582CT/TT and *ABCB1* 3435TT (OR 1.46, 95 % CI 1.02–2.09, *p* *=* 0.040; OR 2.31, 95 % CI 1.33–4.02, *p* *=* 0.003, respectively). Discontinuation due to adverse events (clinician decision) was independently associated with dose (OR 2.54, 95 % CI 1.19–5.43, *p* *=* 0.016). C_12_ between 0.47 and 0.76 mg/L provided sensitivity/specificity >90 % (100 %/92.3 to 98.9 %/92.3 %) for achieving pVL <200 copies/mL at 96 weeks.
54:| EFV-related adverse events and discontinuations due to these events were increased with dose but the higher rate of EFV-related adverse events for EFV600 was not associated with the SNPs investigated. CNS adverse events were not driven by EFV dose or concentrations; however, CYP2B6 15582CT/TT and ABCB1 3435TT carriers were at higher risk (46 and 131 %, respectively) of CNS-related adverse events compared with 35 % lower risk in CYP2B6 983TC/CC patients. Possession of the CYP2B6 516GT and TT variants and CYP2A6*9B CA/AA carriers was associated with a higher risk of overall EFV discontinuation (80, 166 and 100 %, respectively), whereas NR1I2 63396TT carriers were at decreased risk (22 %). |
76:The SNPs *CYP2B6* 516 G>T (rs3745274), *CYP2B6* 983 T>C (rs28399499), *CYP2B6* 15582C>T (rs4803419), *CYP2A6**9B (rs8192726), *CYP2A6**17 (rs28399454), *CYP3A4**22 (rs35599367), *NR1I3* 540C>T (rs2307424) and *NR1I3* 1089T>C (rs3003596) were previously genotyped [[5](#CR5)]. Additionally, *ABCB1* 3435C>T (rs1045642), *NR1I2* 63396C>T (rs2472677) and *NR1I2* 7635A>G (rs6785049) were genotyped using real-time PCR allelic discrimination assays for the present analysis (C_7586657_20, C26079845_10 and C_29280426_10, respectively; Applied Biosystems, Foster City, CA, USA), as previously described [[8](#CR8), [9](#CR9)].
133:| CYP2B6 15582C>T |  |  |  |
219:| CYP2B6 15582C>T |  |  |  |
254:Following adjustment for age, sex and dose, and stratifying by country, *CYP2B6* 516GT, TT and *CYP2A6**9B heterozygote or homozygous variant (CA or AA) patients had an 80, 166 and 100 % increased risk of overall discontinuation, respectively, whereas *NR1I2* 63396TT carriers had a 22 % reduced risk (Table [4](#Tab4)). Upon multivariable Cox regression analysis, dose or SNPs were not associated with EFV-related adverse events (Stocrin^®^ product information or clinician decision) following adjustment; however, a greater risk of stopping due to EFV-related adverse events by clinician decision was observed with EFV600 compared with EFV400 (OR 2.54, 95 % CI 1.19–5.43, *p* *=* 0.016). A decreased risk of CNS adverse events (Stocrin^®^ product information) was associated with *CYP2B6* 983TC or CC carriers (OR 0.30, 95 % CI 0.12–0.75, *p* *=* 0.010) but an increased risk in patients with *CYP2B6* 15582CT or TT and *ABCB1* 3435TT carriers was observed (OR 1.59, 95 % CI 1.11–2.27, *p* *=* 0.011; and OR 2.14, 95 % CI 1.25–3.67, *p* *=* 0.006, respectively).
268:| CYP2B6 15582C>T |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |
313:Possession of homozygous wild-type *CYP2B6* 15582C>T/516G>T/983T>C (CC/GG/TT) is predictive of EFV C_24_ in the lowest concentration stratum [[15](#CR15)], and concerns have grown as to whether this population of individuals would be at increased risk of virological failure, particularly when receiving EFV400. This genotype was not predictive of failure in patients receiving the standard EFV dose [[14](#CR14)] and 47 ENCORE1 patients randomised to EFV400 with this genotype; only one had a detectable pVL ≥200 copies/mL at 96 weeks. Individual mean predicted EFV C_24_ was 2.79 mg/L in this patient and well above the median of 0.82 mg/L for this genotype group.

CNS adverse events were not driven by EFV dose or concentrations; however, CYP2B6 15582CT/TT and ABCB1 3435TT carriers were at higher risk (46 and 131 %, respectively) of CNS-related adverse events compared with 35 % lower risk in CYP2B6 983TC/CC patients. Possession of the CYP2B6 516GT and TT variants and CYP2A6*9B CA/AA carriers was associated with a higher risk of overall EFV discontinuation (80, 166 and 100 %, respectively), whereas NR1I2 63396TT carriers were at decreased risk (22 %). |
76:The SNPs *CYP2B6* 516 G>T (rs3745274), *CYP2B6* 983 T>C (rs28399499), *CYP2B6* 15582C>T (rs4803419), *CYP2A6**9B (rs8192726), *CYP2A6**17 (rs28399454), *CYP3A4**22 (rs35599367), *NR1I3* 540C>T (rs2307424) and *NR1I3* 1089T>C (rs3003596) were previously genotyped [[5](#CR5)]. Additionally, *ABCB1* 3435C>T (rs1045642), *NR1I2* 63396C>T (rs2472677) and *NR1I2* 7635A>G (rs6785049) were genotyped using real-time PCR allelic discrimination assays for the present analysis (C_7586657_20, C26079845_10 and C_29280426_10, respectively; Applied Biosystems, Foster City, CA, USA), as previously described [[8](#CR8), [9](#CR9)].
133:| CYP2B6 15582C>T |  |  |  |
219:| CYP2B6 15582C>T |  |  |  |
254:Following adjustment for age, sex and dose, and stratifying by country, *CYP2B6* 516GT, TT and *CYP2A6**9B heterozygote or homozygous variant (CA or AA) patients had an 80, 166 and 100 % increased risk of overall discontinuation, respectively, whereas *NR1I2* 63396TT carriers had a 22 % reduced risk (Table [4](#Tab4)). Upon multivariable Cox regression analysis, dose or SNPs were not associated with EFV-related adverse events (Stocrin^®^ product information or clinician decision) following adjustment; however, a greater risk of stopping due to EFV-related adverse events by clinician decision was observed with EFV600 compared with EFV400 (OR 2.54, 95 % CI 1.19–5.43, *p* *=* 0.016). A decreased risk of CNS adverse events (Stocrin^®^ product information) was associated with *CYP2B6* 983TC or CC carriers (OR 0.30, 95 % CI 0.12–0.75, *p* *=* 0.010) but an increased risk in patients with *CYP2B6* 15582CT or TT and *ABCB1* 3435TT carriers was observed (OR 1.59, 95 % CI 1.11–2.27, *p* *=* 0.011; and OR 2.14, 95 % CI 1.25–3.67, *p* *=* 0.006, respectively).
268:| CYP2B6 15582C>T |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |   |
313:Possession of homozygous wild-type *CYP2B6* 15582C>T/516G>T/983T>C (CC/GG/TT) is predictive of EFV C_24_ in the lowest concentration stratum [[15](#CR15)], and concerns have grown as to whether this population of individuals would be at increased risk of virological failure, particularly when receiving EFV400. This genotype was not predictive of failure in patients receiving the standard EFV dose [[14](#CR14)] and 47 ENCORE1 patients randomised to EFV400 with this genotype; only one had a detectable pVL ≥200 copies/mL at 96 weeks. Individual mean predicted EFV C_24_ was 2.79 mg/L in this patient and well above the median of 0.82 mg/L for this genotype group.

steps.message:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final answer. The key findings are:

**Relevant papers (4 clearly relevant, using star allele notation in the body text):**
1. PMC3394799 - CYP2B6\*1, \*6 - efavirenz, drug-induced liver injury (DILI), CNS toxicity
2. PMC3680019 - CYP2B6\*6 - efavirenz, neuropsychiatric symptoms
3. PMC4405819 - CYP2B6\*6, \*18 - efavirenz, HIV/AIDS, CNS adverse effects
4. PMC5065384 - CYP2B6\*1, \*6 - efavirenz, QTc prolongation, torsade de pointes

PMC12370162 uses only SNP notation in its body text (516G>T, c.485-18C>T) — star alleles only appear in cited reference titles. This is borderline but should be counted as relevant since the SNPs (516G>T = defining SNP of \*6; c.485-18C>T = rs4803419 = defining SNP of \*38) directly correspond to the variants of interest and the paper is specifically studying CYP2B6 pharmacogenomics for efavirenz.

steps.message:
Here is a summary of my findings:

## Results for CYP2B6\*1, \*2, \*6, \*18, \*38

**Relevant papers: 5 out of 16**

| Paper | Variants | Key Findings |
|-------|----------|--------------|
| PMC3394799 | \*1, \*6 | CYP2B6\*6 associated with DILI (HR 2.54) in efavirenz-treated HIV patients |
| PMC3680019 | \*6 | CYP2B6\*6/\*6 homozygotes had significantly higher rates of neuropsychiatric symptoms (vivid dreams, insomnia, hallucinations) on efavirenz |
| PMC4405819 | \*6, \*18 | CYP2B6\*6 and \*18 together explained 55% of inter-individual EFV clearance variability; dose-reduction guidance |
| PMC5065384 | \*1, \*6 | CYP2B6\*6/\*6 carriers exceeded FDA QTcF threshold for QTc prolongation; gene-dose effect demonstrated; torsade de pointes risk |
| PMC12370162 | \*6 (516G>T), \*38 (c.485-18C>T) | PopPK-PGx model showing reduced EFV clearance with both SNPs; CNS toxicity risk cited |

**Drugs:** efavirenz (the only drug consistently associated across all relevant papers)

**Phenotypes:** drug-induced liver injury, neuropsychiatric symptoms, QTc interval prolongation, torsade de pointes, CNS toxicity

Note: CYP2B6\*2 and \*38 (by star-allele name) were not explicitly mentioned in any paper body text. CYP2B6\*1 appeared as the wild-type reference. The SNP rs4803419 (c.485-18C>T / 15582C>T) that defines \*38 appears in PMC12370162 and PMC4916189 but without the star-allele label.
